---
title: "NV Control Framework"
page-layout: full
toc: false
---


## Modular Python framework for experimental control of diamond NV center systems

A modern, modular software framework for controlling and automating nitrogen-vacancy (NV) center experiments. The framework is designed around hardware abstraction, reusable experiment modules, and scalable software engineering principles to support long-term development of quantum sensing platforms.

<div class="hero-buttons">

[Architecture](architecture.qmd){.btn .btn-primary}
[Hardware](hardware.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-primary}
[Experiments](experiments.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-primary}
[Project Roadmap](roadmap.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-primary}

</div>

---

## Project at a Glance

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-6 .g-col-lg-3}

### Status

🟢 Active Development

:::

::: {.g-col-6 .g-col-lg-3}

### Language

Python

:::

::: {.g-col-6 .g-col-lg-3}

### Architecture

Modular Object-Oriented Design

:::

::: {.g-col-6 .g-col-lg-3}

### Platform

Cross Platform

:::

:::

---

## Overview

The NV Control Framework provides a unified software architecture for laboratory automation in diamond NV center experiments. It separates experiment logic from hardware implementation, enabling new instruments and measurement protocols to be integrated with minimal changes to the overall codebase.

The framework currently supports widefield and confocal NV experiments and is designed to scale toward advanced quantum sensing applications, including real-time control with Quantum Machines OPX and high-speed imaging platforms.

---

## Design Philosophy

The framework is built around five core principles:

- Modular hardware abstraction
- Reusable experiment modules
- Configuration-driven initialization
- Scalable software architecture
- Documentation-driven development

---

## Current Capabilities

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}

::: {.project-card}

### Instrument Control

- Scientific Cameras
- Microwave Sources
- Motion Stages
- Pulse Streamers
- Power Supplies

:::

:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}

::: {.project-card}

### Experiment Modules

- ODMR
- Widefield ODMR
- Automation Framework
- Data Acquisition
- Experiment Management

:::

:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}

::: {.project-card}

### Software Infrastructure

- Hardware Abstraction
- JSON Configuration
- Modular Architecture
- Git Version Control
- Automated Testing

:::

:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}

::: {.project-card}

### Planned Features

- Rabi
- Ramsey
- T₁ / T₂
- Microwave-Free Imaging
- Quantum Machines OPX

:::

:::

:::

---

## Software Architecture

The framework follows a layered architecture that separates user interaction, experiment logic, hardware abstraction, configuration management, and the physical experimental platform.

![](assets/architecture_v1.png){width=100% fig-alt="NV Control Framework Architecture"}

*Figure 1. Architecture of the NV Control Framework (Version 1.0).*

---

## Documentation

Additional documentation is available in the following sections.

| Section | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| **Architecture** | Software architecture and design philosophy |
| **Hardware** | Supported instruments and drivers |
| **Experiments** | Measurement modules and workflows |
| **Roadmap** | Planned development milestones |

---

## Continue Exploring

- [Architecture](architecture.qmd)
- [Hardware](hardware.qmd)
- [Experiments](experiments.qmd)
- [Roadmap](roadmap.qmd)
- [← Research Portfolio](../index.qmd)
